title: Artifact Type
description:
Definition of an artifact type. Artifacts are structured work products (JSON-serializable data) produced and consumed by agents. Studios define their own artifact types.

VALIDATION STRATEGY: The 'fields' array uses ordered field definitions optimized for LLM prompting. The Runtime MUST compile these into standard JSON Schema Draft 2020-12 for validation. This 'transpiler' approach trades engineering complexity for LLM ergonomics. For edge cases requiring advanced JSON Schema features (oneOf, allOf, patternProperties), use 'json_schema_override'.

RESERVED SYSTEM FIELDS: Field names starting with underscore (_) are reserved for Runtime use. Studios MUST NOT define fields with these names. The Runtime manages: _id (instance identifier), _type (artifact type ref), _version (semver), _created_at, _updated_at, _created_by (agent ref), _lifecycle_state (if lifecycle defined). The _lifecycle_state field is write-protected and can only be modified via lifecycle_transition_request messages.

id.description: Unique identifier for this artifact type

description.description: What this artifact represents and when it is used

summary.description: Concise one-liner for artifact type menus. Use description for full explanation.

category.description: General category of artifact. 'manifest' indicates this artifact references external assets.

fields.description: Ordered field definitions. Order is preserved when presenting to LLMs. Runtime compiles to JSON Schema for validation.

json_schema_override.description: Optional raw JSON Schema fragment merged into the compiled schema. Use for advanced features not expressible in field_definition (oneOf, allOf, patternProperties, etc.). Merged AFTER field compilation, so can override/extend.

lifecycle.description: Optional lifecycle states and transitions for this artifact type

lifecycle_policy.description: Declarative edit/mutation policies for runtime enforcement. Defines what happens when artifacts are edited in non-initial states.

validation.description: Additional validation rules beyond field types

default_store.description: Default store where artifacts of this type are persisted

extends.description: Another artifact type this type extends (inherits fields from)

terminal.description: If true, artifact cannot leave this state

states.description: Possible states an artifact of this type can be in

initial_state.description: State assigned when artifact is first created

allowed_agents.description: Agents allowed to make this transition (empty = any)

requires_validation.description: IDs of quality criteria that must pass for this transition

target_store.description: Store to migrate artifact to on this transition. Generalizes cold migration - any transition can trigger store migration. Runtime updates both _lifecycle_state and _store atomically.

lifecycle_policy.description: Declarative policies for runtime enforcement of lifecycle behavior. These policies are enforced automatically by the runtime, reducing cognitive load on agents.

edit_policy.description: Runtime behavior when artifact content is edited in non-initial states. 'allow': edit proceeds normally (default). 'demote': edit succeeds but artifact is auto-transitioned to demote_target_state. 'disallow': edit is rejected with error.

demote_trigger_states.description: States that trigger the edit_policy when set to 'demote' or 'disallow'. Defaults to all states except initial_state. Explicitly list states to customize (e.g., ['review', 'cold'] but not 'approved').

demote_target_state.description: State to transition to when demotion is triggered. Defaults to lifecycle.initial_state (typically 'draft').

demote_target_store.description: Store to migrate artifact to on demotion. Defaults to artifact type's default_store (typically 'workspace').

required_together.description: Groups of fields that must all be present if any one is present

mutually_exclusive.description: Groups of fields where only one may be present
